name: Corredor del Oso Loop
name_original: Corredor del Oso – circular desde Sena de Luna (Parque Natural de Babia y Luna)
description: This moderate 19.9-mile mountain bike route in Parque Natural de Babia y Luna offers technical sections and reservoir views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sena De Luna offers diverse terrain within the Cantabrian Mountains, characterized by mountain passes, gorges, and varied topography. This region, part of the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna, features challenging climbs and descents through natural reserves. The landscape includes significant elevation changes and natural features like the Luna Reservoir, providing a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Sena De Luna The most popular mountain bike trail is Laguna de las Verdes, Laguna la Recoleta y Valle de Valverde – circular desde San Emiliano por la Reserva de la biosfera de Babia, a 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Babia Biosphere Reserve with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás (Reservas de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga), a difficult 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route includes the Aralla Pass, offering challenging climbs and scenic views through the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Local mountain bikers also love the Cruz de San Salvador y Cuito Negro – circular desde el Embalse de Casares por el Alto Bernesga, a 22.6 miles (36.3 km) trail leading through the Alto Bernesga region, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Sena De Luna is defined by mountain passes, gorges, and the varied terrain of the Cantabrian Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Sena De Luna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 170 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sena De Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Sena De Luna?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Sena De Luna, offering a wide range of experiences within the Cantabrian Mountains. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sena De Luna?
A: The best time to visit Sena De Luna for mountain biking is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant, making for ideal riding conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While many trails are moderate to difficult, some sections or shorter routes can be suitable for families. The region's natural routes, such as the Sendero Sena de Luna - Pobladura, offer family-friendly hiking options, and similar terrain can be found for less demanding bike rides. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037686663/corredor-del-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor del Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32 km long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sena De Luna?
A: Mountain bike trails in Sena De Luna feature diverse terrain, including significant elevation changes, mountain passes, and gorges. You'll navigate varied topography within the Cantabrian Mountains, from challenging climbs and descents to scenic paths through natural reserves.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Sena De Luna are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035356061/laguna-de-las-verdes-laguna-la-recoleta-y-valle-de-valverde-circular-desde-san-emiliano-por-la-reserva-de-la-biosfera-de-babia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Verdes, Laguna la Recoleta y Valle de Valverde – circular desde San Emiliano por la Reserva de la biosfera de Babia&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Babia Biosphere Reserve, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037722733/puerto-aralla-collado-collarriondo-y-hayedo-de-la-boyariza-circular-desde-cubillas-de-arbas-reservas-de-la-biosfera-del-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás&lt;/a&gt; through the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt;, known for its breathtaking views, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/859306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto Negro Summit&lt;/a&gt; also offer impressive vistas. When water levels are low, the 'ghost town' remnants of the old village submerged in the Luna Reservoir become visible, offering a unique historical sight.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area features natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;, which is located within the Los Calderones Gorge. While the gorge itself might require some navigation over rocks, it's a prominent natural enclave worth exploring.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sena De Luna?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sena De Luna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, challenging ascents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Cantabrian Mountains and biosphere reserves.

Q: Are there any routes that include significant mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Sena De Luna is known for its mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037722733/puerto-aralla-collado-collarriondo-y-hayedo-de-la-boyariza-circular-desde-cubillas-de-arbas-reservas-de-la-biosfera-del-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, includes the Aralla Pass, offering challenging climbs and panoramic views. The La Cubilla Pass is also a key highlight for mountain bikers, forming part of the TransCantábrica BTT route.

Q: Can I find routes that start directly from Sena De Luna?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to start from the municipality itself. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Slope of the Torre River – Torre de Babia loop from Sena de Luna&lt;/a&gt; is a 47.2 km circular trail that begins directly from Sena De Luna.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural interest alongside its natural beauty. You can find religious buildings such as the unique rock sanctuary, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, or the 15th-century Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Pruneda. The Parish Church of Sena de Luna and examples of stately homes in the village also offer cultural insights.
tips: Technical section popularly known as the Corridor of the Bear by the most fond of mountain biking. Eminently for livestock use, and with sections of steep slopes, we will have to set foot on land at some point while we crown successive hills. The views of the Barrios de Luna reservoir are great.

Impressive views of the mountains of the Cantabrian Mountains from the La Raya pass located at an altitude of 1,522 meters where the well-known path from Abelgas to Mallo runs. The terrain should not cause us any difficulty if we ride a BTT.
Picturesque forest track on the path from Abelgas to Mallo that runs near the Cuartero stream.

faqs.question: Is the Corredor del Oso trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a hard mountain bike ride. It demands good fitness and advanced riding skills due to technical sections and steep slopes. Riders should be prepared to dismount and push their bikes on some portions.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite challenging, featuring technical sections with steep slopes and rugged, unpaved paths. Historically used for livestock, the trail can be demanding, requiring advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Corredor del Oso trail from Sena de Luna?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Sena de Luna, the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas may vary, but the village serves as the main access point for the trailhead.

faqs.question: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking landscapes within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna. You'll get great views of the Barrios de Luna reservoir and pass by notable elevations like Picos Albos, Peña Orniz, and Peña Ubiña. Don't miss the Corredor del Oso itself and the scenic Collado de la Raya.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the vegetation is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow and ice, making conditions difficult or impassable.

faqs.question: Is the Corredor del Oso trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it's located within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna, which is a Biosphere Reserve and Special Protection Area for Birds, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural de Babia y Luna is known for its diverse ecosystems. While sightings are sporadic, the area is a habitat for various wildlife, including the Cantabrian brown bear. You might also spot various bird species, as it's a Special Protection Area for Birds.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino de Irede al Cuartero, the Camino de Mallo a Abelgas, and another segment of the Camino de Mallo a Abelgas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this challenging trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and technical nature, you should bring appropriate safety gear, including a helmet, gloves, and knee pads. Carry enough water, snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device or a downloaded map is also highly recommended, as some sections can be remote.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near Sena de Luna?
faqs.answer: Sena de Luna, being the starting point, offers some basic amenities. You can find local cafes, restaurants, and rural accommodation options in the village or nearby towns within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna, perfect for before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: Path from Abelgas to Mallo, near the Cuartero stream
